title: Today's Markets Brief: After-Hours Stock Movers
summary: A quick look at the latest stock market news

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to your evening markets brief. U.S. stocks ended the day on a high note, with the Dow Jones Industrial Average up 0.36% at close of trade. Now, let's dive into some of the stocks making moves after hours.

First up, CRM, also known as Salesforce, is seeing some action. I'm looking at reports that the company's stock is on the move after hours, but details are a bit scarce. My read is that investors are reacting to recent earnings expectations, possibly adjusting their valuation of the company. It's always interesting to see how investors respond to these expectations, and it will be great to see how this plays out in the coming days.

Next, I've got my eye on MRVL, or Marvell Technology, which is also making waves. The semiconductor company has been on a bit of a rollercoaster lately, and it seems investors are keeping a close watch on its performance. A quick aside - I recall comparing MRVL to a hyperactive stock on a caffeine high, but that's a story for another time. The company's been making some interesting moves in the semiconductor space, and it's definitely one to keep an eye on.
After-Hours Stock Movers: CRM, MRVL, SNOW, HPQ, DDOG, MDB, P, SNPS, BRZE, NCNO is also worth a quick mention. The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

U.S. stocks higher at close of trade; Dow Jones Industrial Average up 0.36% is also worth a quick mention. The main content says: U.S. stocks higher at close of trade; Dow Jones Industrial Average up 0.36% The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

My quick read is that today's set of stories is useful because it gives us texture, not a sermon. Each item has its own little job: one tells us what changed, another tells us what people are worried about, and another gives the day its mood.
So that is the brief version: a few headlines, a little context, and just enough connective tissue to make the day easier to follow without turning the whole thing into homework.
